title: Bounded replay window integrity

primary_sources.relevance: PerformanceEntry timestamps belong to a performance timeline; PerformanceObserver delivery is queued, buffered delivery is bounded, droppedEntriesCount reports lost observations, takeRecords drains queued records, and supported entry types are runtime-detectable.
Defines Event Timing entries and interaction timing fields used by the existing R-009 summary.
Defines Long Animation Frame entries used by the existing R-010 summary.

observed: R-011 validates a shared replay label and aggregate completeness but does not establish same navigation, same clock domain, or sufficiently overlapping collection windows.
PerformanceObserver callbacks are scheduled by the user agent, buffered delivery is finite, takeRecords drains pending records, and droppedEntriesCount exposes known loss.
Six deterministic Node.js tests passed locally before commit.
finding: Cross-observer fusion must fail closed unless both summaries declare the same navigation identity and time-origin token, report drained buffers with zero known drops, and cover a strongly overlapping bounded interval.
design_implication: Run this validator before R-011. Only packets with window_integrity=true may enter the gesture replay evidence combiner; a matching replay string alone is correlation metadata, not proof of shared observation scope.

cases: same-navigation same-clock strongly overlapping drained windows accepted
undrained buffers and dropped entries rejected
non-overlapping and weakly overlapping windows rejected
raw entries, DOM targets, event names, and script attribution excluded from output

heuristic.status: project threshold, not a standards-defined cutoff

privacy_boundary: Stores opaque replay, navigation, and time-origin tokens plus aggregate interval bounds. It excludes raw PerformanceEntry objects, DOM targets, selectors, event names, script URLs, page text, private chat, health data, and credentials.
uncertainty: The 0.9 overlap threshold is a project heuristic.
Opaque navigation and time-origin tokens must be generated consistently by the runtime; this artifact does not prescribe a cryptographic identity scheme.
Same-window integrity prevents accidental cross-window fusion but does not prove causal attribution.
Target-browser support and observer delivery behavior remain runtime questions.
falsification_route: Collect Event Timing and Long Animation Frame summaries from intentionally different navigations and require rejection.
Offset observer start or stop boundaries until overlap drops below the threshold and require rejection.
Leave pending records undrained or simulate dropped entries and require unresolved output.
Run both observers over one controlled gesture interval and compare accepted bounds against local raw entries without persisting those entries.
Vary the overlap threshold and measure false acceptance and false rejection against controlled replay fixtures.
